2026-04-27.content:
**Generally available**: Cloud TPU now offers TPU availability in AI
zones. To learn more, see
[About AI zones](https://docs.cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/ai-zones).

summary.title: TPU now available in AI zones
summary.summary: Cloud TPU is now generally available in AI zones, providing enhanced capabilities for machine learning workloads. This expansion aims to improve performance and accessibility for users leveraging TPUs for AI tasks. For more details, explore the documentation on AI zones.

2026-03-31.content:
**Generally available**: TPU7x is generally available (GA). TPU7x is the first
release within the Ironwood family, Google Cloud's seventh generation TPU. TPU7x
supports large-scale AI training and inference, providing performance and
cost-effectiveness for demanding workloads such as large language (LLMs),
mixture of experts (MoEs), and diffusion models. For more information, see the
[TPU7x (Ironwood) documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/tpu/docs/tpu7x).

summary.title: TPU7x Now Generally Available
summary.summary: TPU7x, the first of Google Cloud's seventh generation TPUs, is now generally available. It is designed for large-scale AI training and inference, offering improved performance and cost-effectiveness for demanding workloads like LLMs and diffusion models.

2025-11-24.content:
**Preview**: TPU7x is available in Preview. TPU7x is the first release within
the Ironwood family, Google Cloud's seventh generation TPU. TPU7x supports
large-scale AI training and inference, providing performance and
cost-effectiveness for demanding workloads such as large language models (LLMs),
mixture of experts (MoEs), and diffusion models. For more information, see the
[TPU7x (Ironwood) documentation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/tpu/docs/tpu7x).

summary.title: TPU7x Now in Preview
summary.summary: TPU7x, the latest generation of Google's Tensor Processing Units, is now available in Preview. This new hardware is designed for large-scale AI training and inference, offering improved performance and cost-efficiency for demanding AI models like LLMs and diffusion models.
